author: śrīla vṛndāvana⋅dāsa ṭhākura
verses:
rāhu⋅kavale indu, parakāśa nāma⋅sindhu,
kalimardana bāje bāṇā
pahu̐ bhela parakāśa, bhuvana caturdaśa,
jaya jaya paḍila ghoṣaṇā

dekhite gaurāṅgacandra
nadīyāra loka⋅, śoka saba nāśala,
dine dine bāḍala ānanda (<eng>refrain</eng>)

dundubhi bāje, śata śaṅkha gāje,
bāje veṇu⋅viṣāṇa
śrī⋅caitanya⋅ṭhākura, nityānanda⋅prabhu,
vṛndāvana⋅dāsa gāna
en_translation:
Just as the moon is swallowed by Rāhu, the ocean of the Name has appeared. The destroyer of Kali sounds His powerful proclamation. The Lord has manifested throughout the fourteen worlds, and a proclamation of "All Glories!" was heard.

Upon seeing Gaurāṅgacandra, the people of Nadīyā had all their sorrow destroyed, and their joy increased day by day.

The dundubhi sounds, hundreds of conch shells resound, and the flute and horn play. Vṛndāvana Dāsa sings of Śrī Caitanya Ṭhākura and Nityānanda Prabhu.
